# Usage and Cost API

Programmatically access your organization's API usage and cost data with the Usage & Cost Admin API.

---

<Tip>
**The Admin API is unavailable for individual accounts.** To collaborate with teammates and add members, set up your organization in **Console → Settings → Organization**.
</Tip>

The Usage & Cost Admin API provides programmatic and granular access to historical API usage and cost data for your organization. This data is similar to the information available in the [Usage](/usage) and [Cost](/cost) pages of the Claude Console.

This API enables you to better monitor, analyze, and optimize your Claude implementations:

* **Accurate Usage Tracking:** Get precise token counts and usage patterns instead of relying solely on response token counting
* **Cost Reconciliation:** Match internal records with Anthropic billing for finance and accounting teams
* **Product performance and improvement:** Monitor product performance while measuring if changes to the system have improved it, or setup alerting
* **[Rate limit](/docs/en/api/rate-limits) and [Priority Tier](/docs/en/api/service-tiers#get-started-with-priority-tier) optimization:** Optimize features like [prompt caching](/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-caching) or specific prompts to make the most of one’s allocated capacity, or purchase dedicated capacity.
* **Advanced Analysis:** Perform deeper data analysis than what's available in Console

<Check>
  **Admin API key required**

  This API is part of the [Admin API](/docs/en/build-with-claude/administration-api). These endpoints require an Admin API key (starting with `sk-ant-admin...`) that differs from standard API keys. Only organization members with the admin role can provision Admin API keys through the [Claude Console](/settings/admin-keys).
</Check>

## Partner solutions

Leading observability platforms offer ready-to-use integrations for monitoring your Claude API usage and cost, without writing custom code. These integrations provide dashboards, alerting, and analytics to help you manage your API usage effectively.

<CardGroup cols={3}>
  <Card title="CloudZero" icon="chart" href="https://docs.cloudzero.com/docs/connections-anthropic">
    Cloud intelligence platform for tracking and forecasting costs
  </Card>
  <Card title="Datadog" icon="chart" href="https://docs.datadoghq.com/integrations/anthropic/">
    LLM Observability with automatic tracing and monitoring
  </Card>
  <Card title="Grafana Cloud" icon="chart" href="https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/monitor-infrastructure/integrations/integration-reference/integration-anthropic/">
    Agentless integration for easy LLM observability with out-of-the-box dashboards and alerts
  </Card>
  <Card title="Honeycomb" icon="polygon" href="https://docs.honeycomb.io/integrations/anthropic-usage-monitoring/">
    Advanced querying and visualization through OpenTelemetry
  </Card>
  <Card title="Vantage" icon="chart" href="https://docs.vantage.sh/connecting_anthropic">
    FinOps platform for LLM cost & usage observability
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Quick start

Get your organization's daily usage for the last 7 days:

```bash
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2025-01-08T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2025-01-15T00:00:00Z&\
bucket_width=1d" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

<Tip>
  **Set a User-Agent header for integrations**

  If you're building an integration, set your User-Agent header to help us understand usage patterns:
  ```text
  User-Agent: YourApp/1.0.0 (https://yourapp.com)
  ```
</Tip>

## Usage API

Track token consumption across your organization with detailed breakdowns by model, workspace, and service tier with the `/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages` endpoint.

### Key concepts

- **Time buckets**: Aggregate usage data in fixed intervals (`1m`, `1h`, or `1d`)
- **Token tracking**: Measure uncached input, cached input, cache creation, and output tokens
- **Filtering & grouping**: Filter by API key, workspace, model, service tier, context window, [data residency](/docs/en/build-with-claude/data-residency), or speed (beta), and group results by these dimensions
- **Server tool usage**: Track usage of server-side tools like web search

For complete parameter details and response schemas, see the [Usage API reference](/docs/en/api/admin-api/usage-cost/get-messages-usage-report).

### Basic examples

#### Daily usage by model

```bash
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2025-01-08T00:00:00Z&\
group_by[]=model&\
bucket_width=1d" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

#### Hourly usage with filtering

```bash
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2025-01-15T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2025-01-15T23:59:59Z&\
models[]=claude-opus-4-6&\
service_tiers[]=batch&\
context_window[]=0-200k&\
bucket_width=1h" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

#### Filter usage by API keys and workspaces

```bash
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2025-01-08T00:00:00Z&\
api_key_ids[]=apikey_01Rj2N8SVvo6BePZj99NhmiT&\
api_key_ids[]=apikey_01ABC123DEF456GHI789JKL&\
workspace_ids[]=wrkspc_01JwQvzr7rXLA5AGx3HKfFUJ&\
workspace_ids[]=wrkspc_01XYZ789ABC123DEF456MNO&\
bucket_width=1d" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

<Tip>
To retrieve your organization's API key IDs, use the [List API Keys](/docs/en/api/admin-api/apikeys/list-api-keys) endpoint.

To retrieve your organization's workspace IDs, use the [List Workspaces](/docs/en/api/admin-api/workspaces/list-workspaces) endpoint, or find your organization's workspace IDs in the Anthropic Console.
</Tip>

#### Data residency

Track your [data residency controls](/docs/en/build-with-claude/data-residency) by grouping and filtering usage with the `inference_geo` dimension. This is useful for verifying geographic routing across your organization.

```bash
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2026-02-01T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2026-02-08T00:00:00Z&\
group_by[]=inference_geo&\
group_by[]=model&\
bucket_width=1d" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

You can also filter to a specific geo. Valid values are `global`, `us`, and `not_available`:

```bash
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2026-02-01T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2026-02-08T00:00:00Z&\
inference_geos[]=us&\
group_by[]=model&\
bucket_width=1d" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

<Note>
Models released before February 2026 (prior to Claude Opus 4.6) don't support the `inference_geo` request parameter, so their usage reports return `"not_available"` for this dimension. You can use `not_available` as a filter value in `inference_geos[]` to target those models.
</Note>

#### Fast mode (beta: research preview)

Track [fast mode](/docs/en/build-with-claude/fast-mode) usage by grouping and filtering with the `speed` dimension. This is useful for monitoring standard vs. fast mode usage.

```bash
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2026-02-01T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2026-02-08T00:00:00Z&\
group_by[]=speed&\
group_by[]=model&\
bucket_width=1d" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "anthropic-beta: fast-mode-2026-02-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

You can also filter to a specific speed. Valid values are `standard` and `fast`:

```bash
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2026-02-01T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2026-02-08T00:00:00Z&\
speeds[]=fast&\
group_by[]=model&\
bucket_width=1d" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "anthropic-beta: fast-mode-2026-02-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

<Note>
Both the `speeds[]` filter and the `speed` group_by value require the `fast-mode-2026-02-01` beta header.
</Note>

### Time granularity limits

| Granularity | Default Limit | Maximum Limit | Use Case |
|-------------|---------------|---------------|----------|
| `1m` | 60 buckets | 1440 buckets | Real-time monitoring |
| `1h` | 24 buckets | 168 buckets | Daily patterns |
| `1d` | 7 buckets | 31 buckets | Weekly/monthly reports |

## Cost API

Retrieve service-level cost breakdowns in USD with the `/v1/organizations/cost_report` endpoint.

### Key concepts

- **Currency**: All costs in USD, reported as decimal strings in lowest units (cents)
- **Cost types**: Track token usage, web search, and code execution costs
- **Grouping**: Group costs by workspace or description for detailed breakdowns. When grouping by `description`, responses include parsed fields like `model` and `inference_geo`
- **Time buckets**: Daily granularity only (`1d`)

For complete parameter details and response schemas, see the [Cost API reference](/docs/en/api/admin-api/usage-cost/get-cost-report).

<Warning>
  Priority Tier costs use a different billing model and are not included in the cost endpoint. Track Priority Tier usage through the usage endpoint instead.
</Warning>

### Basic example

```bash
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/cost_report?\
starting_at=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2025-01-31T00:00:00Z&\
group_by[]=workspace_id&\
group_by[]=description" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

## Pagination

Both endpoints support pagination for large datasets:

1. Make your initial request
2. If `has_more` is `true`, use the `next_page` value in your next request
3. Continue until `has_more` is `false`

```bash
# First request
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2025-01-31T00:00:00Z&\
limit=7" \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"

# Response includes: "has_more": true, "next_page": "page_xyz..."

# Next request with pagination
curl "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages?\
starting_at=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z&\
ending_at=2025-01-31T00:00:00Z&\
limit=7&\
page=page_xyz..." \
  --header "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  --header "x-api-key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

## Common use cases

Explore detailed implementations in [Claude Cookbook](https://platform.claude.com/cookbooks):

- **Daily usage reports**: Track token consumption trends
- **Cost attribution**: Allocate expenses by workspace for chargebacks
- **Cache efficiency**: Measure and optimize prompt caching
- **Budget monitoring**: Set up alerts for spending thresholds
- **CSV export**: Generate reports for finance teams

## Frequently asked questions

### How fresh is the data?
Usage and cost data typically appears within 5 minutes of API request completion, though delays may occasionally be longer.

### What's the recommended polling frequency?
The API supports polling once per minute for sustained use. For short bursts (e.g., downloading paginated data), more frequent polling is acceptable. Cache results for dashboards that need frequent updates.

### How do I track code execution usage?
Code execution costs appear in the cost endpoint grouped under `Code Execution Usage` in the description field. Code execution is not included in the usage endpoint.

### How do I track Priority Tier usage?
Filter or group by `service_tier` in the usage endpoint and look for the `priority` value. Priority Tier costs are not available in the cost endpoint.

### What happens with Workbench usage?
API usage from the Workbench is not associated with an API key, so `api_key_id` will be `null` even when grouping by that dimension.

### How is the default workspace represented?
Usage and costs attributed to the default workspace have a `null` value for `workspace_id`.

### How do I get per-user cost breakdowns for Claude Code?

Use the [Claude Code Analytics API](/docs/en/build-with-claude/claude-code-analytics-api), which provides per-user estimated costs and productivity metrics without the performance limitations of breaking down costs by many API keys. For general API usage with many keys, use the [Usage API](#usage-api) to track token consumption as a cost proxy.

## See also
The Usage and Cost APIs can be used to help you deliver a better experience for your users, help you manage costs, and preserve your rate limit. Learn more about some of these other features:

- [Admin API overview](/docs/en/build-with-claude/administration-api)
- [Admin API reference](/docs/en/api/admin)
- [Pricing](/docs/en/about-claude/pricing)
- [Prompt caching](/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-caching) - Optimize costs with caching
- [Batch processing](/docs/en/build-with-claude/batch-processing) - 50% discount on batch requests
- [Rate limits](/docs/en/api/rate-limits) - Understand usage tiers
- [Data residency](/docs/en/build-with-claude/data-residency) - Control inference geography
```
</details></tool-use>

Excellent! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the answer.

## Cost Control on the Anthropic Claude API

Based on official documentation, here's how to set spending limits and prevent runaway costs:

---

### 1. **Console Spending Limits (UI Path)**

**Location:** `Console.anthropic.com` → **Settings** → **Limits**

In the **Spend limits** section:
- Click **Set spend limit** (if no limit is set)
- Click **Change Limit** (if a limit already exists)

You can set a custom spend limit **lower than your tier's maximum ceiling** to enforce cost controls.

**Workspace-level limits:** Settings → Workspaces → [Select Workspace] → Limits tab

---

### 2. **Claude Code CLI Cost Controls**

**No `--max-budget-usd` flag exists.** Claude Code doesn't have a built-in CLI cost limit flag.

**However, you can:**
- Use `/cost` command in Claude Code to track token usage and costs in real-time
- Track per-session costs with `/cost` 
- Use `/clear` to start fresh and avoid accumulating expensive context
- Set a **workspace spend limit** in the Console (applies to all Claude Code usage under that workspace)

When you first authenticate Claude Code with your Claude Console account, a **"Claude Code" workspace is automatically created**. This workspace centrally tracks all Claude Code usage.

---

### 3. **Per-API-Key Spending Limits**

**Not directly enforced per key.** However:

- The **Usage & Cost API** (Admin API) allows you to filter and track spending **by API key ID**
- You can group costs by `api_key_id` to see which keys are consuming the most
- Use [observability partners](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/usage-cost-api) (Datadog, CloudZero, Vantage, Grafana Cloud, Honeycomb) to set per-key alerts and limits
- For enterprise deployments, use **LiteLLM** (open-source, unaffiliated with Anthropic) to enforce [virtual key spend tracking](https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/proxy/virtual_keys#tracking-spend)

---

### 4. **What Happens When You Hit the Limit**

**Hard-stop behavior:**
- Your API requests will be **rejected** once you hit the organization-level tier ceiling
- You cannot continue using the API until the next calendar month (unless you upgrade to a higher tier)

**Custom spend limit behavior:**
- Set a limit lower than your tier's ceiling to have an intermediate stop point
- Same hard-stop: requests fail when the custom limit is reached

**No "warn and continue" mode** — it's a hard block.

---

### 5. **Other Cost Control Mechanisms**

| Mechanism | How It Works |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Rate Limits (TPM/RPM)** | Throttle token/request volume; prevent bulk overage. Set per workspace in Console Settings → Workspaces |
| **Usage Alerts** | Set up notifications via the [Usage & Cost API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/usage-cost-api) or observability integrations (Datadog, Vantage, etc.) |
| **Workspace Limits** | Set separate spend and rate limits per workspace to isolate teams/projects |
| **Batch Processing Discount** | Use [batch API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/batch-processing) for 50% cost reduction on non-urgent tasks |
| **Prompt Caching** | Reuse cached prompts to reduce input token costs |
| **Model Selection** | Use Sonnet instead of Opus for routine tasks (lower cost, good performance) |
| **Admin API Tracking** | Query `/v1/organizations/usage_report/messages` and `/v1/organizations/cost_report` for fine-grained cost monitoring by model, workspace, or API key |

---

### 6. **Organization Usage Tiers**

Anthropic uses **4-tier spend limits** based on account verification and history:

- **Tier 1-2:** Lower limits ($5-100+ deposits)
- **Tier 3:** ~$1,000 monthly spend capacity
- **Tier 4:** Highest tier for enterprise (~$400+ cumulative deposit)

You can request tier advancement through the Console; larger organizations may require capacity reviews.

---

### Quick Setup for Cost Prevention

1. Go to **Console → Settings → Limits** and set a custom spend limit below your tier ceiling
2. Monitor costs with `/cost` in Claude Code or the **Cost page** in Console
3. Set workspace-level rate limits (TPM/RPM) to throttle concurrent usage
4. (Optional) Set up alerts via [Datadog](https://docs.datadoghq.com/integrations/anthropic/), [CloudZero](https://docs.cloudzero.com/docs/connections-anthropic), or [Vantage](https://docs.vantage.sh/connecting_anthropic) for real-time spend tracking

Sources:
- [Manage costs effectively - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs)
- [Usage and Cost API - Claude API Docs](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/usage-cost-api)
- [Rate limits - Claude API Docs](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/rate-limits)
- [Claude Console Settings/Limits](https://platform.claude.com/settings/limits)
- [Creating and managing Workspaces in the Claude Console](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/9796807-creating-and-managing-workspaces-in-the-claude-console)
- [Claude API Quota Tiers and Limits Explained: Complete Guide 2026](https://www.aifreeapi.com/en/posts/claude-api-quota-tiers-limits)

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-03 13:24:19Z)**_

Yes, there's a hard cap you can set in the Console UI. Here's the short version:

### Set a hard spending limit

1. Go to **console.anthropic.com** -> **Settings** -> **Limits**
2. Click **Set spend limit** (or **Change Limit** if one exists)
3. Set it to whatever you're comfortable with (e.g. $50/mo, $100/mo)

**It's a hard stop** — when you hit it, API requests get rejected. No "warn and continue." The droplet's Claude Code will simply stop working until the next billing cycle or you raise the limit.

### Additional controls worth setting up

| Control | Where | What it does |
|---------|-------|-------------|
| **Spend limit** | Console -> Settings -> Limits | Hard cap on monthly spend |
| **Workspace limits** | Console -> Workspaces -> [workspace] -> Limits | Isolate the droplet's spend from your local usage |
| **Rate limits (TPM/RPM)** | Console -> Workspaces -> Limits | Throttle requests per minute |
| **`/cost` command** | Inside Claude Code | Check spend in real-time during a session |

### Recommendation for your setup

Create a **separate workspace** in the Console for your droplet workers. That way:
- Local Claude Code usage has its own budget
- Remote workers have a separate, capped budget
- If a worker goes haywire, it can't eat into your personal spend

You can use a different API key per workspace, so the droplet gets its own key with its own ceiling.

---

### Current permissions in settings.json

**File tools (auto-allowed):**
- `Read`, `Edit`, `Grep`

**Bash commands (auto-allowed):**
- `git *`, `gh *`, `npm run *`, `npm install *`, `npm test *`, `npm run build *`
- `node *`, `npx *`, `python3 *`
- `curl *`, `find *`, `cd *`, `sleep *`

- `mcp__clickup__*`, `mcp__Sentry__*`, `mcp__digitalocean__*`

### Step 1: Authenticate doctl

Run this in your terminal (it's interactive so I can't do it for you):

1. Go to https://cloud.digitalocean.com/account/api/tokens
2. Click **Generate New Token**
3. Give it **read+write** scope
4. Copy and paste it when prompted

### Step 2: Once doctl is working, run the skill

```
/tools:droplet-clone --build
```

This builds the golden image first. After that, every future deploy is just:

```
/tools:droplet-clone
```

Go ahead and run `! doctl auth init` — I'll pick up from there once you're authenticated.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-03 13:33:13Z)**_

For the DO API token, it needs:

**Full Access (Read + Write)** on these scopes:

| Scope | Why |
|-------|-----|
| **Droplets** | Create, destroy, snapshot workers |
| **Images/Snapshots** | Create and list golden images |
| **SSH Keys** | Register your key with DO |
| **Tags** | Tag droplets as `claude-worker` |
| **Account** | Verify auth works (`doctl account get`) |

When you create the token at https://cloud.digitalocean.com/account/api/tokens:

- **Token name:** `claude-worker` (or whatever you want)
- **Expiration:** Your call — 90 days is reasonable, no expiration if you prefer
- **Scopes:** Select **Full Access** (simplest) or **Custom Scopes** with the above if you want to be precise

The token only lives on your local machine in doctl's config. It never gets sent to the droplet.

Ready when you are — run `! doctl auth init` and paste the token.

That token didn't validate. A couple common causes:

1. **Copied a Personal Access Token but pasted an OAuth token** (or vice versa) — make sure you're generating from the **API** section, not OAuth
2. **Token already expired or was revoked**
3. **Extra whitespace** got copied with the token

Try generating a fresh one:

1. Go to https://cloud.digitalocean.com/account/api/tokens
2. Click **Generate New Token**
3. Name: `claude-worker`
4. Expiration: your choice
5. Scopes: **Full Access**
6. Click **Generate Token**
7. **Copy it immediately** (it only shows once)

Then run again:

```
! doctl auth init
```

When it says `Enter your access token:`, paste the token carefully — no leading/trailing spaces.

The `!` prefix might not be passing through the interactive prompt properly. Two options:

**Option A:** Pass the token directly on the command line:
```
! doctl auth init --access-token YOUR_TOKEN_HERE
```

**Option B:** Open a separate terminal tab and run it there:
```bash
doctl auth init
```

That way you'll see the interactive `Enter your access token:` prompt and can paste it.

Option B is better since it avoids putting the token in your command history here. Once it validates, come back and tell me and we'll continue.

_**User (2026-04-03 13:36:13Z)**_

Clone this Claude Code environment to a new DigitalOcean droplet.

Two modes:
1. **`--build`** — Create a golden image (snapshot) with all tools pre-installed. Run once.
2. **Default** — Deploy a new worker from the golden image in one command. Secrets injected via cloud-init.

## Arguments

Parse `--build` for options:
- `--build` : build the golden image (run once, then deploy from it)
- `--name` : droplet name (default: `claude-worker-{timestamp}`)
- `--size` : droplet size slug (default: `s-4vcpu-8gb`)
- `--region` : region slug (default: `nyc1`)
- `--ssh-key` : path to SSH private key (default: `~/.ssh/id_rsa_do`)
- `--api-key` : Anthropic **Console API key** (sk-ant-...) — NOT a Max/Pro subscription. Will prompt if not provided
- `--gh-token` : GitHub personal access token (will prompt if not provided)
- `--netlify-token` : Netlify auth token (optional, will prompt)
- `--no-remote` : skip starting claude remote-control service
- `--repos` : comma-separated GitHub repos to clone (default: `falconerdean/pm-skills`)
- `--destroy` : list and destroy previous claude-worker droplets
- `--list` : list all active claude-worker droplets
- `--images` : list available claude-worker snapshots

---

## Pre-flight (both modes)

**Assume fresh setup. Walk through each gate — don't skip ahead.**

#### 1. Check doctl installed

```bash
which doctl
```

If missing:
> doctl is not installed. Run: `! brew install doctl`

Stop and wait.

#### 2. Authenticate doctl

```bash
doctl account get --format Email,Status --no-header
```

If 401 or error:
> Your DigitalOcean API token is missing or expired.
> 1. Go to https://cloud.digitalocean.com/account/api/tokens
> 2. Create a token with **read+write** scope
> 3. Run: `! doctl auth init` and paste the token

Stop and wait. Re-check after they confirm.

#### 3. SSH key exists locally

```bash
test -f "$SSH_KEY" && echo "EXISTS" || echo "MISSING"
```

If missing, check available keys: `ls ~/.ssh/*.pub`
If none: suggest `! ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/id_rsa_do -N "" -C "claude-worker"`

Stop and wait.

#### 4. SSH key registered with DO

```bash
LOCAL_FP=$(ssh-keygen -l -E md5 -f "$SSH_KEY.pub" | awk '{print }' | sed 's/MD5://')
doctl compute ssh-key list --format FingerPrint --no-header | grep -q "$LOCAL_FP"
```

If not registered:
```bash
doctl compute ssh-key import "claude-worker-$(date +%s)" --public-key-file "$SSH_KEY.pub"
```

Capture the fingerprint.

---

## Mode 1: `--build` (Create Golden Image)

This builds a snapshot with everything pre-installed EXCEPT secrets. Run once — future deploys use this image.

### Show plan and confirm

```
## Building Golden Image

This will:
1. Create a temporary droplet (s-4vcpu-8gb, ~--build.07/hr)
2. Install all tools and config
3. Snapshot it as "claude-worker-golden-{date}"
4. Destroy the temporary droplet

The snapshot costs --build.05/GB/mo (~/mo for a 20GB image).
Proceed?
```

Wait for confirmation.

### Create temporary builder droplet

```bash
doctl compute droplet create "claude-builder-$(date +%s)" \
  --size s-4vcpu-8gb \
  --region "$REGION" \
  --image ubuntu-24-04-x64 \
  --ssh-keys "$SSH_KEY_FINGERPRINT" \
  --tag-name claude-builder \
  --wait \
  --format ID,PublicIPv4 \
  --no-header
```

Capture **builder ID** and **IP**.

### Wait for SSH

Poll until ready (max 2 min, every 10s):
```bash
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes \
  -i "$SSH_KEY" root@"$BUILDER_IP" echo "ready" 2>/dev/null
```

### Run image provisioning script

Create `/tmp/claude-golden-provision.sh` and SCP + execute it on the builder:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Golden Image Build ==="

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get update -qq
apt-get upgrade -y -qq
apt-get install -y -qq \
  git curl build-essential tmux jq ufw fail2ban \
  unzip wget software-properties-common nano

# --- Swap (2GB) ---
fallocate -l 2G /swapfile
chmod 600 /swapfile
mkswap /swapfile
swapon /swapfile
echo '/swapfile none swap sw 0 0' >> /etc/fstab

# --- Firewall ---
ufw default deny incoming
ufw default allow outgoing
ufw allow 22/tcp
ufw --force enable

# --- Node.js 20 ---
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
apt-get install -y -qq nodejs

# --- Claude Code ---
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

# --- GitHub CLI ---
curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
  | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
  | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list > /dev/null
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y -qq gh

# --- Netlify CLI ---
npm install -g netlify-cli

# --- doctl ---
DOCTL_VERSION="1.114.0"
curl -fsSL "https://github.com/digitalocean/doctl/releases/download/v${DOCTL_VERSION}/doctl-${DOCTL_VERSION}-linux-amd64.tar.gz" \
  | tar -xzf - -C /usr/local/bin

# --- Create claude user ---
useradd -m -s /bin/bash -G sudo claude
echo "claude ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" > /etc/sudoers.d/claude

# --- claude user directory structure ---
su - claude -c bash <<'USEREOF'
mkdir -p ~/.claude/commands ~/.claude/skills ~/.claude/projects ~/.claude/plans ~/projects

# Clone slash commands
git clone https://github.com/wshobson/commands.git ~/.claude/commands

# Git defaults
git config --global init.defaultBranch main
USEREOF

# --- systemd service TEMPLATE (secrets get injected by cloud-init at deploy) ---
cat > /etc/systemd/system/claude-remote.service <<'SYSTEMD'
[Unit]
Description=Claude Code Remote Control
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=claude
WorkingDirectory=/home/claude/projects
EnvironmentFile=/home/claude/.claude/.env
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/claude remote-control --bare --name "%H" --verbose
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
SYSTEMD

# Enable but don't start (no secrets yet)
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable claude-remote.service

# --- Clean up for snapshot ---
apt-get clean
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*
> /var/log/wtmp
> /var/log/btmp
history -c

echo "=== Golden image ready for snapshot ==="
echo "Claude Code: $(claude --version)"
echo "Node.js: $(node --version)"
echo "gh: $(gh --version | head -1)"
echo "netlify: $(netlify --version)"
```

```bash
scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" /tmp/claude-golden-provision.sh root@"$BUILDER_IP":/tmp/
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" root@"$BUILDER_IP" "bash /tmp/claude-golden-provision.sh"
```

#### Skills

```bash
tar -czf /tmp/claude-skills.tar.gz -C ~/.claude/skills .
```

#### CLAUDE.md

Read `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and write to `/tmp/claude-droplet-claude-md`.

#### Memory

```bash
tar -czf /tmp/claude-memory.tar.gz -C ~/.claude/projects/-Users-deanfalconer-GitHub-pm-skills/memory . 2>/dev/null || true
```

#### Upload all

```bash
scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" \
  /tmp/claude-droplet-settings.json root@"$BUILDER_IP":/home/claude/.claude/settings.json

scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" \
  /tmp/claude-droplet-claude-md root@"$BUILDER_IP":/home/claude/.claude/CLAUDE.md

scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" \
  /tmp/claude-skills.tar.gz root@"$BUILDER_IP":/tmp/
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" root@"$BUILDER_IP" \
  "tar -xzf /tmp/claude-skills.tar.gz -C /home/claude/.claude/skills"

# Memory (if exists)
if [ -f /tmp/claude-memory.tar.gz ]; then
  scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" \
    /tmp/claude-memory.tar.gz root@"$BUILDER_IP":/tmp/
  ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" root@"$BUILDER_IP" \
    "mkdir -p /home/claude/.claude/projects/default/memory && tar -xzf /tmp/claude-memory.tar.gz -C /home/claude/.claude/projects/default/memory"
fi

# Fix ownership
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" root@"$BUILDER_IP" \
  "chown -R claude:claude /home/claude"
```

### Power off and snapshot

```bash
# Must power off before snapshot
doctl compute droplet-action power-off "$BUILDER_ID" --wait

# Create snapshot
doctl compute droplet-action snapshot "$BUILDER_ID" \
  --snapshot-name "claude-worker-golden-$(date +%Y%m%d)" \
  --wait

# Get the snapshot ID
SNAPSHOT_ID=$(doctl compute snapshot list --resource droplet \
  --format ID,Name --no-header | grep "claude-worker-golden" | head -1 | awk '{print }')

# Destroy the builder
doctl compute droplet delete "$BUILDER_ID" --force
```

### Report

```
## Golden Image Built

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Snapshot ID | {snapshot_id} |
| Snapshot Name | claude-worker-golden-{date} |
| Cost | ~/mo storage |

### What's baked in
- Ubuntu 24.04 + 2GB swap + UFW + fail2ban
- Node.js 20 + npm
- Claude Code {version}
- GitHub CLI, Netlify CLI, doctl
- tmux, jq, nano
- `claude` user (non-root, sudo)
- {N} slash commands ({N} tools + {N} workflows)
- {N} skills (deep-research, product-pipeline, startup-engine)
- settings.json, CLAUDE.md, memory
- systemd service template (starts on deploy)

### Deploy a worker from this image
\`\`\`
/tools:droplet-clone
\`\`\`
That's it. It will find the golden image automatically.

### Rebuild the image
Run `/tools:droplet-clone --build` again to create a new snapshot
(e.g., after updating commands or skills).
```

---

## Mode 2: Deploy (default — one-line deploy)

### Collect secrets

Ask the user for secrets not provided via flags:
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (required) — Must be a **Console API key** (`sk-ant-...`) from console.anthropic.com. NOT a Max/Pro subscription. The API key ensures: commercial ToS compliance, no OAuth fallback, no risk of account shutdown for automated usage, and pay-per-use billing.
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` (required)
- `NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN` (optional)

**Validate the key format:** If the provided key doesn't start with `sk-ant-`, warn the user that it may be a subscription token and ask them to confirm.

### Find golden image

```bash
SNAPSHOT_ID=$(doctl compute snapshot list --resource droplet \
  --format ID,Name,CreatedAt --no-header | grep "claude-worker-golden" | sort -k3 -r | head -1 | awk '{print }')
```

If no snapshot found:
> No golden image found. Run `/tools:droplet-clone --build` first to create one.

Stop and wait.

### Generate cloud-init user-data

Create `/tmp/claude-cloud-init.sh`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# --- Inject secrets ---
cat > /home/claude/.claude/.env <<'ENVFILE'
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=__ANTHROPIC_KEY__
GH_TOKEN=__GH_TOKEN__
GITHUB_TOKEN=__GH_TOKEN__
NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN=__NETLIFY_TOKEN__
DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1
DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATES=1
DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING=1
DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND=1
HOME=/home/claude
PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/home/claude/.local/bin
ENVFILE

# Replace placeholders
sed -i "s|__ANTHROPIC_KEY__|$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY|g" /home/claude/.claude/.env
sed -i "s|__GH_TOKEN__|$GH_TOKEN|g" /home/claude/.claude/.env
sed -i "s|__NETLIFY_TOKEN__|$NETLIFY_TOKEN|g" /home/claude/.claude/.env

chmod 600 /home/claude/.claude/.env
chown claude:claude /home/claude/.claude/.env

# --- Shell profile ---
cat >> /home/claude/.bashrc <<PROFILE
# Claude Code environment
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"
export GH_TOKEN="$GH_TOKEN"
export GITHUB_TOKEN="$GH_TOKEN"
export NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN="$NETLIFY_TOKEN"
export DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1
export DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATES=1
export DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING=1
export DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND=1
export EDITOR=nano
export PATH="\$HOME/.local/bin:\$PATH"
PROFILE

# --- Copy SSH keys to claude user ---
mkdir -p /home/claude/.ssh
cp /root/.ssh/authorized_keys /home/claude/.ssh/
chown -R claude:claude /home/claude/.ssh
chmod 700 /home/claude/.ssh
chmod 600 /home/claude/.ssh/authorized_keys

# --- Git config ---
su - claude -c 'git config --global user.name "Claude Worker"'
su - claude -c 'git config --global user.email "claude-worker@$(hostname).do"'

# --- GitHub auth ---
if [ -n "$GH_TOKEN" ]; then
  su - claude -c "echo '$GH_TOKEN' | gh auth login --with-token" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# --- Clone repos ---
__REPO_CLONE_BLOCK__

# --- Start remote control ---
systemctl start claude-remote.service
```

**Before writing the file**, replace the placeholders:
- `$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `$GH_TOKEN`, `$NETLIFY_TOKEN` with actual values
- `__REPO_CLONE_BLOCK__` with a line per repo:
  ```bash
  su - claude -c "cd ~/projects && gh repo clone OWNER/REPO -- --depth 1" || true
  ```

### Show plan and confirm

```
## Ready to deploy

| Setting | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Name | {name} |
| Image | claude-worker-golden-{date} (snapshot {id}) |
| Size | {size} (~${cost}/mo) |
| Region | {region} |
| SSH Key | {fingerprint} |
| Repos | {repo_list} |
| Remote Control | {yes/no} |

Secrets: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (set), GITHUB_TOKEN (set), NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN ({set/skipped})

Proceed?
```

Wait for confirmation.

### Create the droplet

```bash
doctl compute droplet create "$DROPLET_NAME" \
  --size "$SIZE" \
  --region "$REGION" \
  --image "$SNAPSHOT_ID" \
  --ssh-keys "$SSH_KEY_FINGERPRINT" \
  --tag-name claude-worker \
  --user-data-file /tmp/claude-cloud-init.sh \
  --wait \
  --format ID,Name,PublicIPv4 \
  --no-header
```

That's it. One command. Cloud-init runs the script on first boot.

### Wait for cloud-init to finish

```bash
# Wait for SSH
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes \
  -i "$SSH_KEY" claude@"$IP" echo "ready" 2>/dev/null

# Check cloud-init status (may take 30-60s after SSH is up)
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" root@"$IP" \
  "cloud-init status --wait"
```

### Verify

```bash
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i "$SSH_KEY" claude@"$IP" bash <<'VERIFY'
echo "=== Verification ==="
echo "Claude Code: $(claude --version 2>/dev/null || echo NOT FOUND)"
echo "Node.js: $(node --version)"
echo "gh: $(gh auth status 2>&1 | head -1)"
echo "Commands: $(ls ~/.claude/commands/tools/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l) tools"
echo "Skills: $(ls ~/.claude/skills/ 2>/dev/null | tr ' ' ', ')"
echo "Settings: $(test -f ~/.claude/settings.json && echo OK || echo MISSING)"
echo "Projects: $(ls ~/projects/ 2>/dev/null | tr ' ' ', ')"
echo "Remote Control: $(systemctl is-active claude-remote.service 2>/dev/null || echo inactive)"
VERIFY
```

### Report

```
## Worker Deployed

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Name | {name} |
| IP | {ip} |
| SSH | `ssh -i {ssh_key} claude@{ip}` |
| Claude | `ssh -i {ssh_key} claude@{ip} -t "claude"` |
| Remote | active (claude-remote.service) |
| Deploy time | {seconds}s |

### Quick start
\`\`\`bash
# Interactive
ssh -i {ssh_key} claude@{ip} -t "claude"

# One-shot
ssh -i {ssh_key} claude@{ip} "claude -p 'your prompt' --output-format json"

# Persistent session
ssh -i {ssh_key} claude@{ip} -t "tmux new -s work"

# Logs
ssh -i {ssh_key} claude@{ip} "sudo journalctl -u claude-remote -f"
\`\`\`
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Golden Image Build ==="

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get update -qq
apt-get upgrade -y -qq
apt-get install -y -qq \
  git curl build-essential tmux jq ufw fail2ban \
  unzip wget software-properties-common nano

# --- Swap (2GB) ---
fallocate -l 2G /swapfile
chmod 600 /swapfile
mkswap /swapfile
swapon /swapfile
echo '/swapfile none swap sw 0 0' >> /etc/fstab
echo "Swap configured"

# --- Firewall ---
ufw default deny incoming
ufw default allow outgoing
ufw allow 22/tcp
ufw --force enable
echo "Firewall configured"

# --- Node.js 20 ---
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
apt-get install -y -qq nodejs
echo "Node $(node --version) installed"

# --- Claude Code ---
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
echo "Claude Code $(claude --version) installed"

# --- GitHub CLI ---
curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
  | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg 2>/dev/null
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
  | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list > /dev/null
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y -qq gh
echo "GitHub CLI installed"

# --- Netlify CLI ---
npm install -g netlify-cli
echo "Netlify CLI installed"

# --- doctl ---
DOCTL_VERSION="1.114.0"
curl -fsSL "https://github.com/digitalocean/doctl/releases/download/v${DOCTL_VERSION}/doctl-${DOCTL_VERSION}-linux-amd64.tar.gz" \
  | tar -xzf - -C /usr/local/bin
echo "doctl installed"

# --- Create claude user ---
useradd -m -s /bin/bash -G sudo claude
echo "claude ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" > /etc/sudoers.d/claude
echo "claude user created"

# --- claude user directory structure ---
su - claude -c 'mkdir -p ~/.claude/commands ~/.claude/skills ~/.claude/projects ~/.claude/plans ~/projects'

# Clone slash commands
su - claude -c 'git clone https://github.com/wshobson/commands.git ~/.claude/commands'
echo "Commands cloned"

# Git defaults
su - claude -c 'git config --global init.defaultBranch main'

# --- systemd service TEMPLATE ---
cat > /etc/systemd/system/claude-remote.service <<'SYSTEMD'
[Unit]
Description=Claude Code Remote Control
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=claude
WorkingDirectory=/home/claude/projects
EnvironmentFile=/home/claude/.claude/.env
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/claude remote-control --bare --name "%H" --verbose
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
SYSTEMD

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable claude-remote.service
echo "systemd service configured"

# --- Clean up for snapshot ---
apt-get clean
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*
> /var/log/wtmp
> /var/log/btmp

echo "=== Golden image ready for snapshot ==="
echo "Claude Code: $(claude --version)"
echo "Node.js: $(node --version)"
echo "npm: $(npm --version)"
echo "gh: $(gh --version | head -1)"

```

### What's baked in
- Ubuntu 24.04 + 2GB swap + UFW firewall + fail2ban
- Claude Code 2.1.91
- Node.js v20.20.2 + npm 10.8.2
- GitHub CLI 2.89.0
- Netlify CLI
- doctl 1.114.0
- tmux, jq, nano
- `claude` user (non-root, sudo)
- 45 slash commands (tools) + 15 workflows
- 3 skills (deep-research, product-pipeline, startup-engine)
- settings.json with 80+ auto-approved permissions
- CLAUDE.md + memory files
- systemd service template (starts on deploy)

### Deploy a worker from this image
```
/tools:droplet-clone
```
That's it. It'll find the golden image, ask for your API keys, and spin up a worker in ~60 seconds.
